input.user_input: How does this lease agreement define 'tenant default' and what remedies does the landlord have?

actual:
It appears you have not uploaded or provided the lease agreement. Please upload the document, and I will gladly review it to explain exactly how it defines a "tenant default" and what specific remedies it grants to the landlord. 

Generally, lease agreements define a tenant default as failing to pay rent on time, breaching other lease covenants (such as unauthorized subletting or causing property damage), or abandoning the premises. Landlord remedies typically include the right to terminate the lease, initiate eviction proceedings, apply the security deposit to unpaid rent or damages, and pursue legal action for the remaining balance. However, the exact definitions and procedures—such as required notice periods and cure rights—vary significantly from contract to contract, so I will need to see your specific agreement to give you an accurate answer.
